Off Calendar Oct-05-2020 Off Calendar

Petition For: (1) Court Order Invalidating Purported Amendment To Trust Based On Lack Of Capacity, Undue Influence And Fraud; (2) Financial Elder Abuse; (3) Conversion; (4) Unjust Enrichment; (5) Recovery Of Personal Property; (6) Declaratory Relief And Constructive Trust; (7) Cancellation Of Instrument; (8) For Double And Punitive Damages;
Off Calendar Mar-25-2020 Continued To Oct-05-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For: (1) Court Order Invalidating Purported Amendment To Trust Based On Lack Of Capacity, Undue Influence And Fraud; (2) Financial Elder Abuse; (3) Conversion; (4) Unjust Enrichment; (5) Recovery Of Personal Property; (6) Declaratory Relief And Constructive Trust; (7) Cancellation Of Instrument; (8) For Double And Punitive Damages;
Off Calendar Mar-09-2020 Continued To Jul-13-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Randy Schmidt for Vicky and Mark Lavell, Respondents.
Timothy Barteau for Robert Gutierrez, Petitioner.

The Court thanked counsel for the joint status statement and stated that having reviewed the joint status statement it will not send the case to the trial department until discovery is complete, and that discovery is not going to go on forever. Mr. Schmidt stated that Mr. Barteau served another round of written discovery to be done in 30 days, that there are depositions set, and they will need several months to get all discovery completed. Mr. Barteau requested for the next status hearing be in July. Mr. Schmidt stated they can work out dates. Both counsels reported the civil matter is stayed.

The petition for order invalidating amendment, etc. is continued to July 13, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for next status conference. Courtesy copy of joint status statement to be delivered by July 6, 2020.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: S. Le. Deputy: K. Tauscher. Matter not reported.

Petition For: (1) Court Order Invalidating Purported Amendment To Trust Based On Lack Of Capacity, Undue Influence And Fraud; (2) Financial Elder Abuse; (3) Conversion; (4) Unjust Enrichment; (5) Recovery Of Personal Property; (6) Declaratory Relief And Constructive Trust; (7) Cancellation Of Instrument; (8) For Double And Punitive Damages;
Off Calendar Feb-10-2020 Continued To Mar-09-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Timothy Barteau for Robert Gutierrez.
Randy Schmidt for Mark and Vicky Lidell, Respondents.

Counsel reported that parties opted for private mediation instead of court's pro bono mediation, that they aren't sure if there is an agreement by parties, that it is pre-mature to set for trial, that both sides propounded discovery, and that there are 7 depositions to be taken all together prior to an evidentiary hearing.

The Petition for order invalidating amendment; etc. is continued to March 09, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Joint status report to be filed by 4pm March 3, 2020.

Judge: Joeseph M. Quinn. Clerk: S. Le. Deputy: K. Tauscher. Matter not reported.

Petition For: (1) Court Order Invalidating Purported Amendment To Trust Based On Lack Of Capacity, Undue Influence And Fraud; (2) Financial Elder Abuse; (3) Conversion; (4) Unjust Enrichment; (5) Recovery Of Personal Property; (6) Declaratory Relief And Constructive Trust; (7) Cancellation Of Instrument; (8) For Double And Punitive Damages;

Petition For: (1) Court Order Invalidating Purported Amendment To Trust Based On Lack Of Capacity, Undue Influence And Fraud; (2) Financial Elder Abuse; (3) Conversion; (4) Unjust Enrichment; (5) Recovery Of Personal Property; (6) Declaratory Relief And Constructive Trust; (7) Cancellation Of Instrument; (8) For Double And Punitive Damages;
Off Calendar Jun-10-2019 Continued To Oct-21-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Timothy Barteau for Robert Gutierrez (Petitioner).
Ciarin O'Sullivan specially appearing for Attorney Randall Schmidt for Respondents.

Mr. O'Sullivan stated that they filed a response on June 5th and that they are amenable to mediation. The Parties agreed to participate in mediation. The Court signed the order referring the matter to Pro Bono Mediation. Mediation must be completed no later than October 8, 2019. The Parties are to notify the Probate Examiner as to the outcome of mediation by October 15, 2019. The Petition for Order Invalidating Purported Amendment to Trust, etc. is continued to and the Parties are to return on October 21, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of mediation.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.
